_comment: Optional airband receiver config for maia-httpd. Copy to /root/airband.json on the Pluto. If absent, maia-httpd uses its built-in defaults, now identical to this file (including gain_db 0). samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 128 / 7 = 15625 sps at 14 MHz). channels_hz: up to 21 absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2. gain: default fixed manual 0 dB. The AD9361's internal gain stage is itself the dominant generator of the conducted spur comb AND broadband noise/intermod -- both grow with internal gain and collapse SFDR. The correct front-end is a clean low-NF EXTERNAL LNA doing the gain ahead of the Pluto with internal gain at its floor (an A/B vs internal gain showed the external LNA is markedly cleaner: lower floor, fewer broadband peaks). This default ASSUMES an external LNA; on a BARE front end (no LNA) 0 dB is very insensitive -- raise gain_db (range [0 .. 77]) for that case, accepting more comb/intermod. See firmware/diagnostics/ and SPUR-INVESTIGATION.md.
